title: EX-0004 remove graybox presentation and ship cohesive pond game
goal: Replace the visibly layered graybox/poster treatment in EX-0004 with one cohesive, polished pond-game presentation. The generated pond artwork must become visibly meaningful atmosphere rather than a dark hidden backing layer, while the exact orthographic square-unit mechanic remains mathematically unambiguous.
acceptance: The intro no longer looks like a graybox embedded in a marketing card: remove the duplicate flat inline-SVG poster, internal EX-0004 identifier, diagnostic boundary copy, unit-key badge, and heavy dark masking; use the unbranded generated pond art as the dominant bright hero image with readable integrated copy.

The active experience reads as the same pond world rather than a separate prototype: replace the crude perimeter SVG props, thick brown frame, frame plaque, boxed equation label, and bottom legend with a restrained game HUD and painterly pond treatment. Generated art may be blurred/cropped/masked decoratively so its perspective lines cannot compete with the exact playable lattice.

The playable lattice remains orthographic, 120 by 100 viewBox units with 10 by 10 true square cells, but grid styling is subtle and game-like rather than spreadsheet-like. The one-cell unit relation stays visible through one elegant in-world cue plus accessible text, not repeated debug labels.

The frog, endpoints, traces, discrepancy, obstacle, and exact landing receive a coherent dimensional visual treatment. The frog remains the direct tutorial control and every purposeful motion/reduced-motion invariant from the prior slice remains intact.

The hierarchy fits one desktop viewport and a 320px touch viewport without the title, board, controls, or feedback feeling like stacked graybox panels; no horizontal overflow or hidden required control occurs.

No logo, trademark, copied title treatment, recognizable branded character, source-game board layout, watermark, or external network asset appears. Generated art remains decorative and all mathematical state remains authored in inspectable HTML/SVG.

A dedicated Playwright visual-structure regression goes red on the current layered graybox treatment and proves the removed artifacts do not return; ordinary, reduced-motion, keyboard, retry-in-place, and 320px smoke paths remain green.
DESIGN.md records why the previous polish still looked like a graybox, the replacement hierarchy, generated-art role, preserved geometry, visual review evidence, and the unchanged efficacy boundary.
must_ask_if: The validated prediction, automatic resolution, scene count, orthogonal transfer, obstacle arithmetic, retry-in-place behavior, or evidence semantics would need to change.
The exact square lattice would need perspective distortion, non-uniform scaling, or replacement by artwork-encoded geometry.
A branded/reference character, copied composition, external dependency, network request, or file outside the declared areas appears necessary.
The generated image cannot be made visually prominent without exposing its own perspective grid as a second mathematical grid.
verify: cd experiments && npx playwright test tests/smoke/ex-0004-visual-polish.spec.ts tests/smoke/ex-0004-round-retry.spec.ts
